name: Exxon Climate Knowledge
description: The concept of 'Exxon Climate Knowledge' refers to the accusation that ExxonMobil (now Exxon) possessed significant internal knowledge about the risks of climate change as early as the 1970s and 1980s, but publicly downplayed or denied those risks while simultaneously using this knowledge for internal business decisions. Proponents argue that Exxon's own scientists conducted sophisticated climate modeling and research which accurately predicted future warming, sea-level rise, and other climate impacts. They claim Exxon then funded climate change denial campaigns and lobbying efforts to obstruct policies aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions, prioritizing short-term profits over long-term environmental consequences. Mainstream scientific consensus overwhelmingly supports the reality of anthropogenic climate change. Investigations by InsideClimate News and the Los Angeles Times in 2015 brought this issue to widespread public attention, based on internal Exxon documents. Exxon has consistently denied these accusations, arguing that its research was part of a broader scientific inquiry, that its positions reflected the uncertainty surrounding climate science at the time, and that it has supported climate research and solutions. Critics point to the documented discrepancies between Exxon's internal research and its public statements as evidence of deliberate deception. The debate continues to be a significant point of contention in discussions about corporate responsibility and climate change policy.
subtitle: Alleged suppression of climate science by Exxon.

claims.claim: Exxon possessed early and accurate climate change knowledge.
claims.evidence: Internal Exxon documents, scientific reports, and memos from the 1970s and 1980s.
claims.criticism: Exxon argues this research was part of broader scientific inquiry and doesn't prove malicious intent.

claims.claim: Exxon deliberately downplayed or denied climate change risks publicly.
claims.evidence: Contradictory public statements, funding of climate change denial groups and lobbying efforts.
claims.criticism: Exxon claims its positions reflected the uncertainty surrounding climate science at the time and that it has supported climate research.

claims.claim: Exxon used its climate knowledge for internal business decisions, such as Arctic drilling planning.
claims.evidence: Internal memos and strategic planning documents referencing climate change impacts on future operations.
claims.criticism: Exxon maintains its business decisions were based on a variety of factors and that acknowledging climate change risks doesn't equate to malicious exploitation.

keyFigures.name: Naomi Oreskes
keyFigures.role: Historian of science, author of 'Merchants of Doubt'

keyFigures.name: Edward G. Rendell
keyFigures.role: Former Governor of Pennsylvania, leading voice on energy policy

keyFigures.name: Lee Raymond
keyFigures.role: Former CEO of ExxonMobil, oversaw period of alleged disinformation

mythologyConnections: Gaea (environmental damage as offense against the Earth)

timeline.event: Exxon funds climate change denial groups and lobbies against climate policies.
InsideClimate News and Los Angeles Times publish investigative reports.
Ongoing legal battles and public debate regarding Exxon's climate knowledge and actions.

sources.author: Neela Banerjee, Lisa Song, John H. Cushman Jr., David Hasemyer
sources.title: Neela Banerjee, Lisa Song, John H. Cushman Jr., David Hasemyer

sources.author: Naomi Oreskes and Erik M. Conway
sources.title: Naomi Oreskes and Erik M. Conway

worshipPractices.name: Denial Ritual
worshipPractices.description: A performative rejection of scientific consensus regarding climate change, often involving strategic obfuscation and the promotion of alternative narratives. This ritual aims to maintain the status quo and protect vested interests by minimizing public concern.

worshipPractices.name: Lobbying Offering
worshipPractices.description: The practice of influencing political decisions through substantial financial contributions and persuasive arguments. This involves cultivating relationships with policymakers to ensure favorable regulations and policies that support continued fossil fuel extraction and consumption.

festivals.name: Shareholder Sanctification
festivals.description: A ceremonial reaffirmation of the corporation's commitment to maximizing shareholder value, even if it means disregarding the long-term environmental consequences. This event reinforces the hierarchy of priorities and justifies environmentally damaging activities in the name of profit.

festivals.name: Greenwashing Gala
festivals.description: A carefully orchestrated effort to present a deceptive image of environmental responsibility through superficial initiatives and misleading claims. This festival serves to appease public criticism and enhance the company's reputation without fundamentally changing its business practices.

cultural.modernLegacy: The legacy of 'Exxon Climate Knowledge' is primarily one of distrust and cynicism towards corporations and their role in environmental issues. It fuels ongoing debates about corporate responsibility, transparency, and the ethical implications of prioritizing profit over planetary health. This narrative continues to inspire investigative journalism, environmental activism, and legal challenges aimed at holding corporations accountable for their contributions to climate change.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Pandora's Box represents knowledge or a force that, once unleashed, causes widespread suffering. Exxon's climate knowledge, when suppressed and acted against, resulted in a delayed response to climate change, causing harm to the environment and future generations.

Mimir's Well held profound wisdom, but Odin had to sacrifice an eye to access it. Exxon possessed valuable climate knowledge (wisdom) but arguably sacrificed the long-term health of the planet (an 'eye') for short-term profit.

The Book of Thoth was said to contain powerful secrets that could grant dominion over the earth, but its misuse could bring about chaos. Exxon's climate knowledge, a form of powerful 'secret,' was arguably used to maintain their dominion over the energy market, leading to environmental chaos.

cross_cultural_parallels.entity: The Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil
cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: The Tree of Knowledge offered knowledge that came with a burden and consequences. Exxon possessed climate knowledge (akin to 'knowing good and evil' regarding fossil fuels), and their actions based on that knowledge led to the consequences of climate change.

associations.name: Serpent
associations.significance: The serpent often represents hidden knowledge and deception, mirroring accusations of Exxon's concealed climate knowledge.

associations.name: Pandora's Box
associations.significance: Opening Pandora's Box unleashed unforeseen consequences, analogous to the long-term repercussions of downplaying climate risks.

associations.name: Tar
associations.significance: Tar, a petroleum byproduct, symbolizes the sticky, inescapable consequences and ethical mire surrounding Exxon's alleged actions.

associations.name: Eclipse
associations.significance: An eclipse represents obscured truth and hidden realities, reflecting the alleged suppression of climate knowledge.

associations.name: Seven
associations.significance: Often associated with cycles and periods of time, symbolizing the decades during which climate knowledge was allegedly suppressed.

associations.name: Oleander
associations.significance: Oleander is a beautiful yet poisonous plant, reflecting the allure of profit masking a dangerous reality.

associations.name: Black
associations.significance: Black symbolizes secrets, hidden agendas, and the darkness associated with withholding crucial information.

companions.name: Apate
companions.description: Apate, the Greek goddess of deceit, would be a natural ally, aiding in the obfuscation and denial of climate science.

companions.name: Jormungandr
companions.description: Jormungandr, the Midgard Serpent of Norse mythology, embodies the long-term, slow-burning threat that climate change represents, coiling around the world.

companions.name: The Hydra
companions.description: The Hydra, with its regenerating heads, symbolizes the persistent and multifaceted nature of misinformation and denial, which requires constant effort to combat.

companions.name: Sirens
companions.description: The Sirens, known for their alluring but deadly songs, represent the tempting promises of short-term economic gain at the expense of long-term environmental sustainability.

companions.name: Loki
companions.description: Loki, the Norse trickster god, offers cunning strategies and deceptive tactics to manipulate public perception and sow doubt about climate science.
